title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded file-admission policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.
Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.
Enforce an explicit allowed-root and symlink policy using resolved ancestry evidence rather than prefix strings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Pathlike Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Bookbot team handles case 8996 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug File Identity
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-file-identity-we0p0q" that uses case 5830 to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug File Identity.

For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug File Identity, keep File Identity connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-file-identity-we0p0q
# Competency: bookbot-file-identity.
# Case 5830: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_file_identity_we0p0q(path: str = "fixtures/story.txt", regular_file: bool = True, within_root: bool = True, bytes_read: int = 4096, byte_limit: int = 65536) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_we0p0q = "we0p0q-q0p0ew-5830"
    assert evidence_variant_we0p0q
    evidence_axes_we0p0q = (
        "corpuswe0p0q",
        "policyq0p0ew",
        "revision5830",
        "commandwe0p0q5830",
        "reportq0p0ew5830",
        "test5830we0p0q",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_we0p0q)) == 6
    if not path.strip() or byte_limit <= 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "path-policy"
    if not regular_file:
        return False, bytes_read, "file-type"
    if not within_root:
        return False, bytes_read, "root-escape"
    if bytes_read < 0 or bytes_read > byte_limit:
        return False, bytes_read, "size-budget"
    assert 0 <= bytes_read <= byte_limit
    return True, bytes_read, "admitted-file"
